career_summary.mathematician: Greatest mathematician of antiquity; anticipated calculus via method of exhaustion
career_summary.physicist: Founder of hydrostatics; discovered buoyancy principle (Archimedes' principle)
career_summary.engineer: Invented Archimedes' screw, compound pulleys, war machines for Syracuse
career_summary.astronomer: Built planetarium devices showing celestial motions

modern_scholarship: T.L. Heath, A History of Greek Mathematics (1931)
M. Clagett, Dictionary of Scientific Biography (1970-1990)
primary_texts: Archimedes Palimpsest (1906 discovery, 10th century copy of lost works)
Surviving treatises in Greek and Latin translation

famous_quotes.quote: Eureka, Eureka. I have found it.
Give me a place to stand and I will move the earth
famous_quotes.context: on the power of the lever

famous_quotes.quote: Things incredible to most men who have not studied mathematics

key_beliefs: Pure mathematics is the only worthy intellectual pursuit
Proof is mandatory — no claim valid without rigorous demonstration
Mechanical method is useful for discovery but not proof
The cosmos follows mathematical laws — mathematics reveals divine architecture
notable_idiosyncrasies: Draws geometric figures in bathwater ashes, on oil-anointed body
Forgot to eat/bathe when absorbed in geometry
Sent unproven theorems to Alexandria to test/peers; inserted false ones when they claimed his work
Requested sphere-and-cylinder on his tomb to honor his greatest mathematical result
